creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business? — Garrett Galow, WorkOS
videos.summary: Studio is an internal workspace that lets users ask natural language questions against Snowflake, Linear, and Notion. The LLM generates reusable declarative JavaScript widgets that call data sources directly, making subsequent runs deterministic and cheap. Reliability features include preflight sequencing to manage context windows, layering rules to prioritize primary sources, and query validation before widget hardcoding.

videos.title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP — Tara Agyemang, Google
videos.summary: WebMCP is a proposed web standard by Google that enables AI agents to interact with websites through structured tools instead of scraping the DOM. It offers two implementation paths: a declarative API using HTML attributes for forms and an imperative API with JavaScript-registered custom tools for complex workflows.

videos.title: Your Attention Is the Bottleneck, Not Your Agents — Zack Proser, WorkOS
videos.summary: Zack Proser presents a framework for sustainable AI-assisted development where human attention is the bottleneck, not agent speed. He proposes a four-layer stack including signal agents for notification management, verification gates for safety, weekly self-improvement loops over conversation history, and biometric feedback integration to prevent developer burnout.

videos.title: Stop Making Models Bigger, Make Them Behave — Kobie Crawford, Snorkel
videos.summary: Kobie Crawford argues that smaller models finetuned with reinforcement learning to use tools correctly outperform much larger models that hallucinate. A 4B Snorkel model trained to query table schemas and self-correct surpassed Qwen 3 235B on a financial query task, with training costing under $500. Transfer from single-table to multi-table reasoning improved performance from 13.9% to 26.6% on FinQA.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is Now BANNED?!
videos.summary: Cole Medin discusses the sudden withdrawal of Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 model following a US government export-control directive allegedly triggered by a claimed jailbreak. The video covers the regulatory decision, performance benchmarks Medin conducted before the model was pulled, and implications for developers building on LLMs.

videos.title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination to Ship AI Agents EASILY
videos.summary: Google's Agents CLI is an open-source tool that streamlines building and deploying AI agents to production. It provides sandboxed code execution, agent identity management, and audit trail capabilities to support production-grade agent deployment.

videos.title: Live AI Q&A + Crushing it in Chess at the Same Time - Come Hang Out!
videos.summary: Cole Medin hosts a live AI Q&A session covering topics like AI coding, agents, Claude Code, and Archon. The stream combines discussion with casual chess play and invites the Dynamous community to participate.

videos.title: Claude Plans, Gemini Designs: The Workflow to Build BEAUTIFUL Frontends
videos.summary: Cole Medin demonstrates a workflow that routes frontend development tasks to different models: Claude Opus for planning and integrations, Gemini 3.5 Flash for UI design. The approach composes two flagship models released the same week to optimize for cost and capability tradeoffs.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: Video explains how to build proactive AI agents and self-improving systems using autonomous loops. Covers techniques for autonomous optimization in ads and SEO, and introduces Loopany as an enabling framework with memory and tool integration capabilities.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses emerging autonomous coding capabilities and tools, covering the /goal feature for agentic workflows, Crewlet framework for multi-agent coordination, and Mission exploration for extended autonomous tasks. The commentary presents these as markers of genuine autonomous coding advancement beyond previous iterations.

videos.title: New AI coding paradiagm - OpenAI Symphony
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses OpenAI Symphony, a new AI coding paradigm that includes harness setup, auto-testing capabilities, and workflow configuration. The video covers how Symphony works, integrates with codebase tooling, and demonstrates practical setup steps.

videos.title: Okay, this unleashed my agent
videos.summary: AI Jason demonstrates free AI agent capabilities including autoresearch, self-evolving mechanisms, and multi-layer memory systems. The video covers Claude Code integration, Hermes self-evolve, and Openclaw skill comparison, positioned as unleashing agent potential.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released Nemotron 3 Nano, Super, and Ultra models. The series employs a hybrid Mamba transformer architecture combined with Latent Mixture of Experts and Mixture of Tokens, differentiating it from other open-source models.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax releases M3, a language model that replaces full attention with MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA). The model incorporates tiled optimizations and continuous KV cache reading to reduce latency during prefill and decode stages of inference.

videos.title: Pi Agent explained in 6min..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code explains Pi, an agent framework that provides a "harness layer" for various use cases. The video compares Pi's architecture and capabilities against other agent products like Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and Antigravity.

videos.title: Huawei makes 1.4nm chip??
videos.summary: Caleb discusses Huawei's announcement of a Tau Scaling Law designed to advance China's semiconductor capabilities amid US-China hardware competition. The video examines the role of EUV and DUV lithography, as well as packaging and logic folding techniques, in closing the technological gap.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: AI and the Job Market in 2026
videos.summary: AWS Developers presents perspectives on job hunting in 2026 when AI screens resumes, emphasizing that networking and personal branding matter more than mass applications. The video argues that junior developers still bring value and that AI will transform rather than eliminate jobs, drawing parallels to historical technological transitions.

videos.title: 5 Tips for Deploying AI Agents to Production
videos.summary: AWS Developers presents five production deployment best practices for AI agents: streaming tool call events for UI responsiveness, layered authentication with API Gateway and Cognito, type-safe contracted database access with tenant isolation, guardrails with cost budgets and model routing, and observability through metrics and OpenTelemetry tracing. Patterns are demonstrated on AWS services but claimed to be generally applicable.

videos.title: Infrastructure as Code for Beginners (AWS CDK)
videos.summary: AWS CDK tutorial demonstrating Infrastructure as Code principles by building a serverless REST API with DynamoDB, Lambda, and API Gateway using Python. Contrasts manual AWS console setup with declarative infrastructure definitions in CDK.

videos.title: Stop AI Hallucinations With These 5 Techniques
videos.summary: A video tutorial presents five techniques to reduce hallucinations in AI agents: semantic tool selection to filter context, graph RAG for precise queries, multi-agent validation for verification, symbolic guardrails enforced in code, and runtime steering for self-correction. Demos use Strands Agents framework on a travel agent application with measurable token savings and failure prevention.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: Alberta Tech discusses whether Google engineers adopt "vibe coding" (code generation via LLMs) and compares approaches at Google to OpenAI Codex and Claude Code. The video examines current and projected future adoption of code generation at Google across 2024-2026.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech discusses Malus, a company or product they claim is "killing open source." The video covers how Malus works and explores the future implications for open source maintainers, with segments on AI agents and open source sustainability.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's software development performance against their own work and found the AI outperformed them. The video explores whether AI tools like Claude Code will replace human developers across all job functions.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Pigment is an AI performance management platform that integrates finance, supply chain, HR, and sales data to help teams make better decisions. Founded by Eléonore Crespo, it has raised over $400 million and serves major customers like Figma, Brex, and Anthropic, growing 2x year over year.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval (a $1B-valued AI platform automating corporate IT), argues that real product-market fit manifests as customer pull rather than founder enthusiasm, requires embedded customer relationships over formal interviews, and demands betting on AI model trajectories rather than current capabilities.

videos.title: He Raised $26M Betting on Probabilities, Not Guesses | Koah Labs, Nic Baird
videos.summary: Nic Baird, founder of Koah Labs, discusses his decision-making framework from competitive sailing applied to startup building. He argues that ad-based monetization is more viable than subscriptions for AI tools and identifies human trust adoption as the primary bottleneck for AI agents rather than technical limitations.

videos.title: I Hit $1M ARR in 117 Days. Bootstrapped to $10M. Here's My Playbook | Chatbase, Yasser Elsaid
videos.summary: Yasser Elsaid, founder of Chatbase, shares insights on bootstrapping to $10M ARR without external funding, achieving $1M ARR in 117 days. The interview covers strategies on product-led growth, SEO, pricing, churn reduction, and decision-making frameworks for scaling.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 Now Banned in Europe by US Government (EU-Breakdown)
videos.summary: A video by Cyril Imhof discusses the claimed ban of Claude Fable 5 in Europe by the US government. The video references regulatory developments concerning Anthropic's model access across EU jurisdictions.

videos.title: The Ultimate Second Brain for AI Entrepreneurs: Fable 5 Claude with Loops
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Fable 5, an Anthropic model designed for AI entrepreneurs, highlighting its features, guardrails, memory capabilities, and agentic payment integration compared to Mythos 5.

videos.title: Anthropic's Brand New AI Just Started the Gold Rush (Fable 5)
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Anthropic's new Claude Fable 5 model and its applications in regulated industries. The video covers new use cases for the model and introduces "Know Your Agent" methodology for effective context engineering in long-running AI applications.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
